mechanism: Credit's negative skewness (-1.2 to -1.8) and excess kurtosis (4-8) mean mean-variance optimization systematically overweights credit, contributing to demand-driven premium compression. Implementation-adjusted Sharpe (0.25-0.35) is half of backtested (0.5-0.7).
regime_dependence: Structural: mean-variance frameworks always miss higher moments. Compression intensifies when inflows from MV-optimizing allocators increase during low-vol regimes.
